meta.name: triz-ai Extended TRIZ Matrix
meta.author: flyersworder (Qing)
meta.notes: 50-parameter extended TRIZ contradiction matrix from the triz-ai project (v0.14.0). Parameters 1-39 are Altshuller's classic engineering parameters; parameters 40-50 extend coverage to modern domains: function efficiency, noise/vibration, harmful emissions, compatibility, security, safety, environmental sustainability, information amount, controllability, scalability, and aesthetics. 40 inventive principles with sub-principles. Matrix contains 2345 populated cells across the full 50x50 space (863 cells involve extended parameters 40-50). Cells for params 40-50 were LLM-seeded by the triz-ai project. IMPORTANT: Validation found that the classic 39x39 region of this matrix matches the canonical Altshuller matrix at only 17.6%. The project documentation claims only parameters 40-50 were LLM-seeded, but the cell data divergence suggests the entire matrix was LLM-generated. Use the classic region with caution — for authoritative classic cells, cross-reference with altshuller_39x39.json.

1.name: Weight of moving object
1.description: The mass of the object in a gravitational field. The force that the body exerts on its support or suspension in the field of gravity.

2.name: Weight of stationary object
2.description: The mass of the object in a gravitational field when the object is not moving. The force that the body exerts on its support or suspension.

3.name: Length of moving object
3.description: Any one linear dimension of the moving object, not necessarily the longest.

4.name: Length of stationary object
4.description: Any one linear dimension of the stationary object, not necessarily the longest.

5.name: Area of moving object
5.description: A geometric characteristic of the moving object described by the part of a plane enclosed by a line. The square measure of the surface of the moving object.

6.name: Area of stationary object
6.description: A geometric characteristic of the stationary object described by the part of a plane enclosed by a line. The square measure of the surface of the stationary object.

7.name: Volume of moving object
7.description: The cubic measure of space occupied by the moving object. Length times width times height for a rectangular object.

8.name: Volume of stationary object
8.description: The cubic measure of space occupied by the stationary object. Length times width times height for a rectangular object.

9.name: Speed
9.description: The velocity of an object; the rate of a process or action in time.

10.name: Force (intensity)
10.description: Force measures the interaction between systems. In Newtonian physics force equals mass times acceleration. In TRIZ force is any interaction that is intended to change an object's condition.

11.name: Stress or pressure
11.description: Force per unit area. Also used for tension.

12.name: Shape
12.description: The external contours, appearance of a system.

13.name: Stability of the object's composition
13.description: The wholeness or integrity of the system; the relationship of the system's constituent elements. Wear, chemical decomposition, and disassembly are all decreases in stability.

14.name: Strength
14.description: The extent to which the object is able to resist changing in response to force. Resistance to breaking.

15.name: Duration of action by a moving object
15.description: The time that the moving object can perform the action. Service life. Mean time between failures for a moving object.

16.name: Duration of action by a stationary object
16.description: The time that the stationary object can perform the action. Service life. Mean time between failures for a stationary object.

17.name: Temperature
17.description: The thermal condition of the object or system. Includes the ability of the system to raise or lower its temperature and the ability to withstand temperature changes.

18.name: Illumination intensity
18.description: Light flux per unit area, measured in lux. Also includes any optical properties of the system such as brightness, color quality, etc.

19.name: Use of energy by moving object
19.description: The energy required to perform a particular action with a moving object. The measure of the object's capacity for doing work.

20.name: Use of energy by stationary object
20.description: The energy required to perform a particular action with a stationary object. The measure of the object's capacity for doing work.

21.name: Power
21.description: The time rate at which work is performed. The rate of use of energy.

22.name: Loss of energy
22.description: The reduction of the energy involved in completing a particular action. Use of energy that does not contribute to the desired function.

23.name: Loss of substance
23.description: Partial or complete, permanent or temporary loss of some of a system's materials, substances, parts, or subsystems.

24.name: Loss of information
24.description: Partial or complete, permanent or temporary loss of data or access to data in or by a system. Includes sensory data such as aroma, texture, etc.

25.name: Loss of time
25.description: Time is the duration of an activity. Improving loss of time means reducing the time taken for the activity.

26.name: Quantity of substance/the matter
26.description: The number or amount of a system's materials, substances, parts, or subsystems which might be altered or would be difficult to alter.

27.name: Reliability
27.description: A system's ability to perform its intended functions in predictable and consistent ways under expected conditions.

28.name: Measurement accuracy
28.description: The closeness of the measured value to the actual value of a property of a system. Reducing the error of measurement increases measurement accuracy.

29.name: Manufacturing precision
29.description: The extent to which the actual characteristics of the system or object match the specified or required characteristics.

30.name: Object-affected harmful factors
30.description: Susceptibility of a system to externally generated harmful effects.

31.name: Object-generated harmful factors
31.description: A harmful effect is one that reduces the efficiency or quality of the functioning of the object or system. Harmful effects generated by the object or system as part of its operation.

32.name: Ease of manufacture
32.description: The degree of facility, comfort, or effortlessness in manufacturing or fabricating the object or system.

33.name: Ease of operation
33.description: Simplicity: the process is not complex, does not require a large number of people or significant effort. Convenience of use.

34.name: Ease of repair
34.description: Quality characteristics such as convenience, comfort, simplicity, and time to repair faults, failures, or defects in a system.

35.name: Adaptability or versatility
35.description: The extent to which a system or object positively responds to external changes. A resistance to change or a system that can be used in multiple ways under a variety of circumstances.

36.name: Device complexity
36.description: The number and diversity of elements and element interrelationships within a system. The user may be unaware of the complexity.

37.name: Difficulty of detecting and measuring
37.description: Measuring or monitoring systems that are complex, costly, require a long time, or are difficult to set up. Making measurements more difficult and less accurate.

38.name: Extent of automation
38.description: The extent to which a system or object performs its functions without human interface. The lowest level of automation includes the use of a manual tool.

39.name: Productivity
39.description: The number of functions or operations performed by a system per unit time. The time for a unit function or operation. The output per unit time, or the cost per unit output.

40.name: Function efficiency
40.description: The ratio of useful function performed to total resources consumed. How well a system converts inputs into desired outputs without waste.

41.name: Noise/vibration (harmful)
41.description: Undesired oscillations or sound produced by a system. Includes mechanical vibration, acoustic noise, electromagnetic interference, and signal noise.

42.name: Harmful emissions
42.description: Undesired outputs released by a system into the environment. Includes chemical emissions, radiation, heat waste, particulates, and pollutants.

43.name: Compatibility/connectability
43.description: The ability of a system to interface, integrate, or work together with other systems, standards, or environments without conflict or degradation.

44.name: Security/vulnerability
44.description: The extent to which a system resists unauthorized access, tampering, data breaches, or exploitation. Includes physical, cyber, and information security.

45.name: Safety
45.description: The extent to which a system prevents harm to users, operators, bystanders, or the environment during normal operation and foreseeable misuse.

46.name: Environmental impact/sustainability
46.description: The total effect of a system on natural ecosystems across its lifecycle. Includes resource depletion, carbon footprint, recyclability, and ecological disruption.

47.name: Amount of information
47.description: The quantity of data a system can store, process, transmit, or present. Includes bandwidth, data density, throughput, and information capacity.

48.name: Controllability/responsiveness
48.description: The ease and precision with which a system's behavior can be directed, adjusted, or regulated. Includes response time, feedback quality, and control granularity.

49.name: Scalability
49.description: The ability of a system to handle growing or shrinking workloads, sizes, or complexity without fundamental redesign or disproportionate resource increase.

50.name: Aesthetics/appearance
50.description: The visual, tactile, or sensory appeal of a system. Includes form factor, surface finish, color, styling, and user-perceived design quality.

1.name: Segmentation
1.description: Divide an object into independent parts, make an object easy to disassemble, or increase the degree of fragmentation or segmentation.
sub_principles: Divide an object into independent parts
Make an object easy to disassemble
Increase the degree of fragmentation or segmentation

2.name: Taking out/Extraction
2.description: Separate an interfering part or property from an object, or single out the only necessary part or property of an object.
sub_principles: Separate an interfering part or property from an object
Single out the only necessary part or property of an object

3.name: Local quality
3.description: Change an object's structure from uniform to non-uniform, change an external environment or external influence from uniform to non-uniform, make each part of an object function in conditions most suitable for its operation, make each part of an object fulfill a different and useful function.
sub_principles: Change an object's structure from uniform to non-uniform
Make each part of an object function in conditions most suitable for its operation
Make each part of an object fulfill a different and useful function

4.name: Asymmetry
4.description: Change the shape of an object from symmetrical to asymmetrical, or if an object is asymmetrical, increase its degree of asymmetry.
sub_principles: Change the shape of an object from symmetrical to asymmetrical
If an object is asymmetrical, increase its degree of asymmetry

5.name: Merging/Combining
5.description: Bring closer together or merge identical or similar objects, assemble identical or similar parts to perform parallel operations, or make operations contiguous or parallel.
sub_principles: Bring closer together or merge identical or similar objects or operations
Assemble identical or similar parts to perform parallel operations
Make operations contiguous or parallel; bring them together in time

6.name: Universality
6.description: Make a part or object perform multiple functions; eliminate the need for other parts.
sub_principles: Make a part or object perform multiple functions
Eliminate the need for other parts

7.name: Nested doll/Nesting
7.description: Place one object inside another; pass one part through a cavity in the other. Make one part pass through a cavity in the other. Telescoping.
sub_principles: Place one object inside another
Make one part pass through a cavity in the other
Use telescoping or retractable components

8.name: Anti-weight/Counterweight
8.description: To compensate for the weight of an object, merge it with other objects that provide lift. To compensate for the weight of an object, make it interact with the environment through aerodynamic or hydrodynamic forces.
sub_principles: Compensate for the weight of an object by merging with another object that provides lift
Compensate for weight by making it interact with the environment (aerodynamic, hydrodynamic, buoyancy forces)

9.name: Preliminary anti-action
9.description: If it will be necessary to do an action with both harmful and useful effects, this action should be replaced with anti-actions to control harmful effects. Create beforehand stresses in an object that will oppose known undesirable working stresses later on.
sub_principles: Perform a counter-action in advance to prevent or neutralize harmful effects
Create preliminary stresses to oppose later undesirable stresses

10.name: Preliminary action
10.description: Perform, before it is needed, the required change of an object (either fully or partially). Pre-arrange objects such that they can come into action from the most convenient place and without losing time for their delivery.
sub_principles: Perform the required change of an object fully or partially in advance
Pre-arrange objects so they can come into action from the most convenient place without losing time

11.name: Beforehand cushioning
11.description: Prepare emergency means beforehand to compensate for the relatively low reliability of an object.
sub_principles: Prepare emergency means beforehand to compensate for low reliability
Add safety margins or backup systems in advance

12.name: Equipotentiality
12.description: In a potential field, limit position changes so that the object need not be raised or lowered. Change the conditions of work so that the object need not be raised or lowered.
sub_principles: Change conditions so that an object does not need to be raised or lowered
Minimize potential energy changes during operation

13.name: The other way round/Inversion
13.description: Invert the action used to solve the problem. Make movable parts fixed, and fixed parts movable. Turn the object or process upside down.
sub_principles: Invert the action or process
Make movable parts fixed, and fixed parts movable
Turn the object or process upside down

14.name: Spheroidality/Curvature
14.description: Instead of using rectilinear parts, surfaces, or forms, use curvilinear ones; move from flat surfaces to spherical ones; use rollers, balls, spirals. Go from linear to rotary motion; use centrifugal forces.
sub_principles: Use curvilinear parts, surfaces, or forms instead of rectilinear ones
Move from flat surfaces to spherical ones; from parts shaped as a cube to ball shapes
Go from linear to rotary motion; use centrifugal forces

15.name: Dynamics
15.description: Allow or design the characteristics of an object, external environment, or process to change to be optimal or to find an optimal operating condition. Divide an object into parts capable of movement relative to each other. If an object is rigid, make it movable or adaptive.
sub_principles: Allow characteristics of an object or process to change to find optimal conditions
Divide an object into parts capable of movement relative to each other
If an object is rigid or inflexible, make it movable or adaptive

16.name: Partial or excessive actions
16.description: If 100 percent of an object is hard to achieve using a given solution method, then by using slightly less or slightly more of the same method, the problem may be considerably easier to solve.
sub_principles: If an exact amount is hard to achieve, use slightly less
If an exact amount is hard to achieve, use slightly more and then remove the excess

17.name: Another dimension
17.description: Move an object in two- or three-dimensional space. Use a multi-story arrangement instead of single-story. Tilt or reorient the object, lay it on its side. Use another side of a given area.
sub_principles: Move an object in two- or three-dimensional space
Use a multi-story arrangement instead of single-story
Tilt or reorient the object; lay it on its side
Use another side of a given area

18.name: Mechanical vibration
18.description: Cause an object to oscillate or vibrate. Increase its frequency. Use an object's resonant frequency. Use piezoelectric vibrators instead of mechanical ones. Use combined ultrasonic and electromagnetic field oscillations.
sub_principles: Cause an object to oscillate or vibrate
Increase the frequency of vibration, even to ultrasonic
Use an object's resonant frequency
Use piezoelectric vibrators instead of mechanical ones

19.name: Periodic action
19.description: Instead of continuous action, use periodic or pulsating actions. If an action is already periodic, change the periodic magnitude or frequency. Use pauses between actions to perform a different action.
sub_principles: Instead of continuous action, use periodic or pulsating actions
If an action is already periodic, change the magnitude or frequency
Use pauses between impulses to perform a different action

20.name: Continuity of useful action
20.description: Carry on work continuously; make all parts of an object work at full load, all the time. Eliminate all idle or intermittent actions or work.
sub_principles: Carry on work continuously; make all parts of an object work at full load all the time
Eliminate all idle or intermittent actions or work

21.name: Skipping/Rushing through
21.description: Conduct a process, or certain stages, at high speed (e.g., a harmful or hazardous process).
sub_principles: Conduct a process or certain stages at high speed
Skip over dangerous or harmful stages quickly

22.name: Blessing in disguise/Turn lemons into lemonade
22.description: Use harmful factors or effects to achieve a positive effect. Eliminate a harmful factor by combining it with another harmful factor. Amplify a harmful factor to such a degree that it is no longer harmful.
sub_principles: Use harmful factors to achieve a positive effect
Eliminate a harmful factor by combining it with another harmful factor
Amplify a harmful factor to such a degree that it is no longer harmful

23.name: Feedback
23.description: Introduce feedback to improve a process or action. If feedback is already used, change its magnitude or influence in accordance with operating conditions.
sub_principles: Introduce feedback to improve a process or action
If feedback is already used, change its magnitude or influence

24.name: Intermediary/Mediator
24.description: Use an intermediary carrier article or intermediary process. Merge one object temporarily with another (which can be easily removed).
sub_principles: Use an intermediary carrier article or process
Merge one object temporarily with another that can be easily removed

25.name: Self-service
25.description: Make an object serve itself by performing auxiliary helpful functions. Use waste resources, energy, or substances.
sub_principles: Make an object serve itself by performing auxiliary helpful functions
Use waste resources, energy, or substances

26.name: Copying
26.description: Instead of an unavailable, expensive, or fragile object, use simpler and inexpensive copies. Replace an object with its optical copy (image). Use scale changes (enlarge or reduce).
sub_principles: Instead of an unavailable, expensive, or fragile object, use simpler and inexpensive copies
Replace an object or process with optical copies
If visible optical copies are used, move to infrared or ultraviolet copies

27.name: Cheap short-living objects
27.description: Replace an inexpensive object with a multiple of inexpensive objects, compromising certain qualities (such as service life).
sub_principles: Replace an expensive object with multiple inexpensive objects
Compromise certain qualities such as service life

28.name: Mechanics substitution
28.description: Replace a mechanical means with a sensory (optical, acoustic, taste or smell) means. Use electric, magnetic, and electromagnetic fields to interact with the object. Change from static to movable fields, from unstructured to structured. Use fields in conjunction with field-activated particles.
sub_principles: Replace a mechanical means with a sensory means (optical, acoustic, etc.)
Use electric, magnetic, and electromagnetic fields to interact with the object
Change from static to movable fields, from unstructured fields to those having structure
Use fields in conjunction with field-activated (ferromagnetic) particles

29.name: Pneumatics and hydraulics
29.description: Use gas and liquid parts of an object instead of solid parts (e.g., inflatable and filled, air cushion, hydrostatic, hydroreactive).
sub_principles: Use gas and liquid parts of an object instead of solid parts
Use inflatable, fluid-filled, air-cushion, hydrostatic, or hydroreactive structures

30.name: Flexible shells and thin films
30.description: Use flexible shells and thin films instead of three-dimensional structures. Isolate the object from the external environment using flexible shells and thin films.
sub_principles: Use flexible shells and thin films instead of three-dimensional structures
Isolate the object from the external environment using flexible shells and thin films

31.name: Porous materials
31.description: Make an object porous or add porous elements (inserts, coatings, etc.). If an object is already porous, use the pores to introduce a useful substance or function.
sub_principles: Make an object porous or add porous elements
If an object is already porous, use the pores to introduce a useful substance or function

32.name: Color changes
32.description: Change the color of an object or its external environment. Change the transparency of an object or its external environment.
sub_principles: Change the color of an object or its external environment
Change the transparency of an object or its external environment
Use colored additives to observe objects or processes that are difficult to see

33.name: Homogeneity
33.description: Make objects interacting with a given object of the same material (or material with identical properties).
sub_principles: Make objects interacting with a given object of the same material
Make interacting parts from material with identical properties

34.name: Discarding and recovering
34.description: Make portions of an object that have fulfilled their functions go away (discard by dissolving, evaporating, etc.) or modify them directly during operation. Restore consumable parts of an object directly in operation.
sub_principles: Make portions of an object that have fulfilled their functions go away
Restore consumable parts of an object directly in operation

35.name: Parameter changes
35.description: Change an object's physical state (e.g., to a gas, liquid, or solid). Change the concentration or consistency. Change the degree of flexibility. Change the temperature.
sub_principles: Change an object's physical state (gas, liquid, solid)
Change the concentration or consistency
Change the degree of flexibility
Change the temperature or volume

36.name: Phase transitions
36.description: Use phenomena occurring during phase transitions (e.g., volume changes, loss or absorption of heat, etc.).
sub_principles: Use phenomena occurring during phase transitions
Exploit volume changes during phase transitions
Exploit heat absorption or release during phase transitions

37.name: Thermal expansion
37.description: Use thermal expansion (or contraction) of materials. If thermal expansion is being used, use multiple materials with different coefficients of thermal expansion.
sub_principles: Use thermal expansion or contraction of materials
Use multiple materials with different coefficients of thermal expansion

38.name: Strong oxidants/Accelerated oxidation
38.description: Replace common air with oxygen-enriched air. Replace enriched air with pure oxygen. Expose air or oxygen to ionizing radiation. Use ionized oxygen (ozone). Replace ozonized (or ionized) oxygen with ozone.
sub_principles: Replace common air with oxygen-enriched air
Replace enriched air with pure oxygen
Expose air or oxygen to ionizing radiation
Use ionized oxygen (ozone)

39.name: Inert atmosphere
39.description: Replace a normal environment with an inert one. Add neutral parts or inert additives to an object.
sub_principles: Replace a normal environment with an inert one
Add neutral parts or inert additives to an object
Carry out the process in a vacuum

40.name: Composite materials
40.description: Change from uniform to composite (multiple) materials.
sub_principles: Change from uniform to composite (multiple) materials
Replace a homogeneous material with a composite one
